name: Shingaling Recipe
brief_description: Shingaling is a popular Filipino street food snack made from seasoned miki noodles that are dried and deep-fried until golden and crispy. This recipe features a savory coating of garlic, chili, and beef powder for an addictive crunch.

instructions.text: In a bowl, combine garlic powder, chili powder, and beef powder. Mix them well.
Rub the mixture on the miki noodles to the point that all sides are coated.
Arrange the noodles in a wide tray. Do not let the noodles overlap each other.
Let the noodles dry under direct sunlight for 1 day. Cover the noodles with a clean screen or cheese cloth to protect it from insects if you are drying them outside of your house.
Heat the oil and deep fry the dried noodles until golden brown.

equipment: Deep fryer or large heavy-bottomed pot
recipe_notes: The drying time may vary depending on humidity and sunlight. The noodles should be completely dry before frying to ensure crispiness.
Be careful when deep frying as the oil may splatter.
You can adjust the amount of chili powder based on your spice preference.
Ensure noodles do not overlap during drying to prevent sticking and uneven drying.
storage_notes: Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1 week.
Do not refrigerate as moisture will make the noodles soggy.
Place a paper towel in the container to absorb any excess oil and maintain crispness.
